title: Lumen — pivot to B2B, or fix the consumer funnel?
subject: Lumen
decision: Should Lumen redirect resources into a B2B corporate-wellness product to chase the enterprise LOIs, or double down on fixing the consumer subscription funnel?
situation:
Lumen — a consumer subscription mobile app for guided meditation and sleep.

Facts available:
- 1.1M total installs; 78,000 paying subscribers; $6.5M ARR.
- Net new paying subscribers have been roughly flat for 3 consecutive quarters (growth plateau).
- Free-trial-to-paid conversion 4.2%, down from 6.1% a year ago. Monthly subscriber churn 8.5%.
- Acquisition is ~80% paid (Meta + TikTok). Blended CAC $42; estimated LTV $61 and compressing as churn rises.
- Content library of ~400 audio sessions; no community or social features; 82% of users on iOS.
- Two inbound letters of intent from employers interested in a corporate-wellness offering, but Lumen has no admin console, seat management, reporting, or SSO.
- Cash: 20 months of runway; 40 employees.

vars.decision: Should Lumen redirect resources into a B2B corporate-wellness product to chase the enterprise LOIs, or double down on fixing the consumer subscription funnel?

note: A consumer-subscription pivot decision — different binding constraint (retention) and a tempting-but-unvalidated B2B escape.
